title: <div><tt>One of the most prominent elements of symbolism from the text would be
content:
One of the most prominent elements of symbolism from the text would be the main character's name. Derived from the myth of the Phoenix - a bird able to rebirth from its own ashes – Phoenix Jackson herself continues to rise above every hurdle she faces. Phoenix fittingly symbolizes a sense of immortality and resilience.

“Her eyes were blue with age. Her skin had a pattern all its own of numberless branching wrinkles and as though a whole little tree stood in the middle of her forehead...” (The Worn Path E. Welty). This quote illustrates the fragility of her old age –which is in stark contrast to her strength to persist on the worn path. Although her body is deteriorating with age, Phoenix continues to push forward; this is her sacrifice.

“She doesn’t come for herself —she has a little grandson. She makes these trips just as regular as clockwork.” (The Worn Path E. Welty). Phoenix's’ devotion and love is apparent, as she has made this arduous journey numerous times for her grandson. By the time she completes her mission of obtaining the medication, her memory starts to lapse. This is symbolistic of not only her physical age, but the mental deterioration due to aging. Although her mind may have momentarily forgotten what compelled her to move forward, her love and determination ensured that she achieved her goal.
